senses.meaningEN: to succeed in reaching a goal

d05-achieve.usageNote: achieve 后直接接目标或成果，不说 achieve to do；表示“设法做成”可用 manage to do。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: A clear study plan can help students achieve better results.
examples.translationCN: 清晰的学习计划能帮助学生取得更好的成绩。
examples.speakText: A clear study plan can help students achieve better results.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Jane Eyre hoped to achieve an independent life through honest work.
examples.translationCN: 简·爱希望通过诚实劳动实现独立生活。
examples.speakText: Jane Eyre hoped to achieve an independent life through honest work.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Several space agencies aim to achieve safer lunar exploration through international cooperation.
examples.translationCN: 近年多个航天机构力求通过国际合作实现更安全的月球探索。
examples.speakText: Several space agencies aim to achieve safer lunar exploration through international cooperation.

senses.meaningEN: to gain knowledge, a skill, or possession

d05-acquire.usageNote: acquire 较正式，常指经过一段过程获得知识、技能或财产。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Students acquire practical skills by taking part in laboratory work.
examples.translationCN: 学生通过参加实验室工作获得实践技能。
examples.speakText: Students acquire practical skills by taking part in laboratory work.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Pip began to acquire the manners he believed a gentleman should possess.
examples.translationCN: 匹普开始学习他认为绅士应有的举止。
examples.speakText: Pip began to acquire the manners he believed a gentleman should possess.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Workers are acquiring new digital skills as artificial intelligence changes many occupations.
examples.translationCN: 随着人工智能改变许多职业，劳动者正在学习新的数字技能。
examples.speakText: Workers are acquiring new digital skills as artificial intelligence changes many occupations.

senses.meaningEN: to request, use, or put something on a surface
senses.pattern: apply for; apply to; apply A to B

d05-apply.usageNote: 申请职位用 apply for a job；向机构申请用 apply to an organization；应用某物用 apply A to B。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: You may apply for the scholarship before the end of April.
examples.translationCN: 你可以在四月底前申请这项奖学金。
examples.speakText: You may apply for the scholarship before the end of April.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Sherlock Holmes would apply careful observation to the smallest clue.
examples.translationCN: 夏洛克·福尔摩斯会把细致观察运用于最微小的线索。
examples.speakText: Sherlock Holmes would apply careful observation to the smallest clue.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Cities are applying heat-mapping data to the design of cooler public spaces.
examples.translationCN: 近年一些城市正把热力测绘数据应用于更凉爽的公共空间设计。
examples.speakText: Cities are applying heat-mapping data to the design of cooler public spaces.

senses.meaningEN: to organize, plan, or put in order

d05-arrange.usageNote: arrange to do 表示安排自己做；arrange for somebody to do 表示安排他人做。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The class representative arranged a meeting with the course tutor.
examples.translationCN: 班代表安排了与课程导师的会面。
examples.speakText: The class representative arranged a meeting with the course tutor.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Mrs Bennet tried to arrange every visit around her daughters' prospects.
examples.translationCN: 班纳特太太试图围绕女儿们的婚姻前景安排每次拜访。
examples.speakText: Mrs Bennet tried to arrange every visit around her daughters' prospects.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Humanitarian groups arrange temporary shelter after severe floods displace families.
examples.translationCN: 严重洪灾导致家庭流离失所后，人道组织会安排临时住所。
examples.speakText: Humanitarian groups arrange temporary shelter after severe floods displace families.

senses.meaningEN: to try to do something difficult; an effort to do it
senses.pattern: attempt to do; an attempt at

d05-attempt.usageNote: 作动词时用 attempt to do；作名词时常用 make an attempt to do 或 an attempt at doing。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The research team attempted to explain the unexpected result.
examples.translationCN: 研究团队试图解释这个意外结果。
examples.speakText: The research team attempted to explain the unexpected result.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Victor Frankenstein's attempt to create life brought consequences he could not control.
examples.translationCN: 维克多·弗兰肯斯坦创造生命的尝试带来了他无法控制的后果。
examples.speakText: Victor Frankenstein's attempt to create life brought consequences he could not control.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Engineers are attempting to recycle more battery materials for electric vehicles.
examples.translationCN: 工程师们正尝试为电动汽车回收更多电池材料。
examples.speakText: Engineers are attempting to recycle more battery materials for electric vehicles.

senses.meaningEN: to draw attention, interest, or movement toward something

d05-attract.usageNote: attract 是及物动词，直接接宾语；be attracted to 表示“被……吸引”。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The science exhibition attracted students from several departments.
examples.translationCN: 这场科学展览吸引了几个院系的学生。
examples.speakText: The science exhibition attracted students from several departments.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Gatsby's bright parties attracted crowds who knew little about their host.
examples.translationCN: 盖茨比灯火辉煌的聚会吸引了许多对主人知之甚少的人。
examples.speakText: Gatsby's bright parties attracted crowds who knew little about their host.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: New battery factories have attracted major investment to several industrial regions.
examples.translationCN: 近年新的电池工厂为多个工业地区吸引了大量投资。
examples.speakText: New battery factories have attracted major investment to several industrial regions.

senses.meaningEN: to prevent something or stay away from it

d05-avoid.usageNote: avoid 后接名词或动名词，不接 to do：avoid making mistakes。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Read the instructions twice to avoid making simple mistakes.
examples.translationCN: 把说明读两遍，以免犯简单错误。
examples.speakText: Read the instructions twice to avoid making simple mistakes.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Odysseus changed his course to avoid dangers that threatened his crew.
examples.translationCN: 奥德修斯改变航线，以避开威胁船员的危险。
examples.speakText: Odysseus changed his course to avoid dangers that threatened his crew.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Health systems use genomic monitoring to avoid being surprised by emerging virus variants.
examples.translationCN: 近年卫生系统利用基因组监测，避免对新出现的病毒变异措手不及。
examples.speakText: Health systems use genomic monitoring to avoid being surprised by emerging virus variants.

senses.meaningEN: to join things so they work or exist together

d05-combine.usageNote: 常用 combine A with B；作不及物动词时可说 two elements combine。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The course combines online lessons with weekly classroom discussions.
examples.translationCN: 这门课程把线上课程与每周课堂讨论结合起来。
examples.speakText: The course combines online lessons with weekly classroom discussions.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Robinson Crusoe combined patience with practical skill to survive on the island.
examples.translationCN: 鲁滨孙把耐心与实用技能结合起来，在岛上生存。
examples.speakText: Robinson Crusoe combined patience with practical skill to survive on the island.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Modern weather models combine satellite observations with powerful computer simulations.
examples.translationCN: 现代天气模型把卫星观测与强大的计算机模拟结合起来。
examples.speakText: Modern weather models combine satellite observations with powerful computer simulations.

senses.meaningEN: to finish making or doing something
having every necessary part

d05-complete.usageNote: complete 作动词后接名词或 doing；作形容词表示“完整的、完全的”。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Each group must complete the survey before presenting its findings.
examples.translationCN: 每组必须在展示调查结果前完成问卷调查。
examples.speakText: Each group must complete the survey before presenting its findings.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Phileas Fogg remained determined to complete his journey around the world.
examples.translationCN: 斐利亚·福格始终决心完成环球旅行。
examples.sourceTitle: Around the World in Eighty Days
examples.speakText: Phileas Fogg remained determined to complete his journey around the world.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Scientists completed the first full draft of the human pangenome reference in recent years.
examples.translationCN: 近年科学家完成了人类泛基因组参考序列的首个完整草图。
examples.speakText: Scientists completed the first full draft of the human pangenome reference in recent years.

senses.meaningEN: to focus attention or gather in one place

d05-concentrate.usageNote: 表示专心于某事用 concentrate on，不用 concentrate in。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Turn off message alerts if you need to concentrate on your essay.
examples.translationCN: 如果你需要专心写论文，就关掉消息提醒。
examples.speakText: Turn off message alerts if you need to concentrate on your essay.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: The old fisherman concentrated on the line despite his pain and exhaustion.
examples.translationCN: 尽管疼痛又疲惫，老渔夫仍把注意力集中在钓线上。
examples.sourceTitle: The Old Man and the Sea
examples.speakText: The old fisherman concentrated on the line despite his pain and exhaustion.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Chip manufacturers are concentrating investment on more energy-efficient designs.
examples.translationCN: 近年芯片制造商正把投资集中于更节能的设计。
examples.speakText: Chip manufacturers are concentrating investment on more energy-efficient designs.

senses.meaningEN: to show or state that something is certain or true

d05-confirm.usageNote: confirm 可接名词或 that 从句；表示“向某人确认”用 confirm something with somebody。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Please confirm your attendance by replying to the email.
examples.translationCN: 请回复邮件确认你会出席。
examples.speakText: Please confirm your attendance by replying to the email.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: A second clue confirmed Holmes's suspicion about the visitor's identity.
examples.translationCN: 第二条线索证实了福尔摩斯对来访者身份的怀疑。
examples.speakText: A second clue confirmed Holmes's suspicion about the visitor's identity.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Telescope observations have confirmed carbon-bearing molecules in distant planetary systems.
examples.translationCN: 近年的望远镜观测已证实遥远行星系统中存在含碳分子。
examples.speakText: Telescope observations have confirmed carbon-bearing molecules in distant planetary systems.

senses.meaningEN: to think carefully about or regard in a particular way
senses.pattern: consider doing; consider A (to be) B

d05-consider.usageNote: 表示“考虑做某事”用 consider doing，不用 consider to do。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Students should consider several sources before forming an opinion.
examples.translationCN: 学生在形成观点前应考虑多个信息来源。
examples.speakText: Students should consider several sources before forming an opinion.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Hamlet considered the cost of action while the court watched him closely.
examples.translationCN: 宫廷密切注视着哈姆雷特，而他思考着行动的代价。
examples.speakText: Hamlet considered the cost of action while the court watched him closely.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Many governments are considering rules for the safe use of generative artificial intelligence.
examples.translationCN: 近年许多政府正在考虑生成式人工智能安全使用规则。
examples.speakText: Many governments are considering rules for the safe use of generative artificial intelligence.

senses.meaningEN: to make something new or cause a situation

d05-create.usageNote: create 强调产生原先不存在的事物或局面；make 的适用范围更广。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The student union created a quiet space for evening study.
examples.translationCN: 学生会为晚间学习设立了一个安静空间。
examples.speakText: The student union created a quiet space for evening study.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Mary Lennox helped create a living garden behind the locked door.
examples.translationCN: 玛丽·伦诺克斯帮助把锁门后的花园变得生机勃勃。
examples.speakText: Mary Lennox helped create a living garden behind the locked door.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Community solar projects are creating lower-cost paths to clean electricity.
examples.translationCN: 近年社区太阳能项目正创造成本更低的清洁用电途径。
examples.speakText: Community solar projects are creating lower-cost paths to clean electricity.

senses.meaningEN: to explain the exact meaning, nature, or limits of something

d05-define.usageNote: define A as B 表示“把 A 定义为 B”；不要与 describe“描述特征”混淆。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The lecturer asked the class to define the key term in one sentence.
examples.translationCN: 讲师要求全班用一句话定义这个关键词。
examples.speakText: The lecturer asked the class to define the key term in one sentence.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: For Elizabeth Bennet, character was defined by judgment rather than wealth.
examples.translationCN: 对伊丽莎白·班纳特而言，品格由判断力而非财富来界定。
examples.speakText: For Elizabeth Bennet, character was defined by judgment rather than wealth.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: New laws are beginning to define how high-risk artificial intelligence systems may be used.
examples.translationCN: 近年的新法律开始界定高风险人工智能系统的使用方式。
examples.speakText: New laws are beginning to define how high-risk artificial intelligence systems may be used.

senses.meaningEN: to say or write what someone or something is like
senses.pattern: describe A as B; describe something to someone

d05-describe.usageNote: 说“向某人描述某物”用 describe something to someone，不用 describe someone something。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Use the diagram to describe how water moves through the system.
examples.translationCN: 请用图表描述水如何流经这个系统。
examples.speakText: Use the diagram to describe how water moves through the system.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Ishmael describes the sea as both a workplace and a mystery.
examples.translationCN: 以实玛利把大海描述成劳作之地，也描述成一个谜。
examples.speakText: Ishmael describes the sea as both a workplace and a mystery.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Climate reports describe longer heat waves across many regions in recent years.
examples.translationCN: 近年的气候报告描述了许多地区持续时间更长的热浪。
examples.speakText: Climate reports describe longer heat waves across many regions in recent years.

senses.meaningEN: to give special importance or attention to something
senses.pattern: emphasize that; emphasize the importance of

d05-emphasize.usageNote: emphasize 是动词；名词是 emphasis，常用 put/place emphasis on。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The professor emphasized the importance of checking every reference.
examples.translationCN: 教授强调了核对每条参考文献的重要性。
examples.speakText: The professor emphasized the importance of checking every reference.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Atticus Finch emphasized fairness even when the town opposed his position.
examples.translationCN: 即使镇上的人反对他的立场，阿蒂克斯·芬奇仍强调公正。
examples.speakText: Atticus Finch emphasized fairness even when the town opposed his position.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Recent public-health campaigns emphasize ventilation as one way to reduce airborne infection.
examples.translationCN: 近年的公共卫生宣传强调，通风是减少空气传播感染的一种方式。
examples.speakText: Recent public-health campaigns emphasize ventilation as one way to reduce airborne infection.

senses.meaningEN: to give support or confidence, or help something develop

d05-encourage.usageNote: encourage 后常用 someone to do；表示促进某事也可直接接名词。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Small discussion groups encourage quieter students to share their ideas.
examples.translationCN: 小组讨论鼓励较安静的学生分享想法。
examples.speakText: Small discussion groups encourage quieter students to share their ideas.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Marmee encouraged her daughters to meet hardship with generosity.
examples.translationCN: 马奇太太鼓励女儿们以慷慨之心面对困境。
examples.speakText: Marmee encouraged her daughters to meet hardship with generosity.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Deposit-return programs encourage consumers to bring bottles back for recycling.
examples.translationCN: 近年押金退还计划鼓励消费者归还瓶子进行回收。
examples.speakText: Deposit-return programs encourage consumers to bring bottles back for recycling.

senses.meaningEN: to inspect, question, or study something carefully

d05-examine.usageNote: examine 强调仔细检查；表示参加考试通常说 take/sit an exam，不说 examine an exam。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The report examines the relationship between sleep and memory.
examples.translationCN: 这份报告研究睡眠与记忆之间的关系。
examples.speakText: The report examines the relationship between sleep and memory.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Holmes examined the worn hat before drawing conclusions about its owner.
examples.translationCN: 福尔摩斯检查了那顶旧帽子，随后对帽主人作出推断。
examples.speakText: Holmes examined the worn hat before drawing conclusions about its owner.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Researchers are examining how microplastics move through food chains and human tissues.
examples.translationCN: 近年研究人员正在考察微塑料如何进入食物链和人体组织。
examples.speakText: Researchers are examining how microplastics move through food chains and human tissues.

senses.meaningEN: to become or make larger in size, range, or amount

d05-expand.usageNote: expand 可及物或不及物；expand on something 表示“进一步详细说明”。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The library plans to expand its collection of electronic journals.
examples.translationCN: 图书馆计划扩充电子期刊馆藏。
examples.speakText: The library plans to expand its collection of electronic journals.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: As Alice changed size, the room seemed to expand and contract around her.
examples.translationCN: 随着爱丽丝的身体变大变小，房间仿佛也在她周围扩张和收缩。
examples.speakText: As Alice changed size, the room seemed to expand and contract around her.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Several countries are expanding offshore wind capacity to diversify their energy supplies.
examples.translationCN: 近年多个国家正在扩大海上风电规模，以实现能源供应多样化。
examples.speakText: Several countries are expanding offshore wind capacity to diversify their energy supplies.

senses.meaningEN: to make or become better

d05-improve.usageNote: improve 可作及物动词“改善某物”，也可作不及物动词“有所改善”。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Regular feedback helped me improve the structure of my writing.
examples.translationCN: 定期反馈帮助我改进了文章结构。
examples.speakText: Regular feedback helped me improve the structure of my writing.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Anne Shirley worked steadily to improve her lessons after each mistake.
examples.translationCN: 安妮·雪莉每次犯错后都踏实努力，以提高功课。
examples.speakText: Anne Shirley worked steadily to improve her lessons after each mistake.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Rapid diagnostic tools are improving the detection of diseases in remote communities.
examples.translationCN: 近年快速诊断工具正在改善偏远社区的疾病检测。
examples.speakText: Rapid diagnostic tools are improving the detection of diseases in remote communities.

senses.meaningEN: to influence or cause a change in someone or something

d06-affect.usageNote: affect 通常是动词“影响”，直接接宾语；effect 通常是名词“影响、结果”，常见 have an effect on。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: A lack of sleep can affect a student's ability to concentrate.
examples.translationCN: 睡眠不足会影响学生的专注能力。
examples.speakText: A lack of sleep can affect a student's ability to concentrate.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: The harsh winter affected every family's hopes in the small town.
examples.translationCN: 严冬影响了小镇上每个家庭的希望。
examples.speakText: The harsh winter affected every family's hopes in the small town.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Extreme heat increasingly affects workers, schools, and transport systems across many regions.
examples.translationCN: 近年极端高温日益影响许多地区的劳动者、学校和交通系统。
examples.speakText: Extreme heat increasingly affects workers, schools, and transport systems across many regions.

senses.meaningEN: a result, change, or influence produced by something

d06-effect.usageNote: effect 通常是名词“结果、影响”，常用 effect on；affect 通常是动词“影响”。不要写成 have an affect on。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The tutoring program had a positive effect on first-year students.
examples.translationCN: 辅导计划对一年级学生产生了积极影响。
examples.speakText: The tutoring program had a positive effect on first-year students.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Scrooge saw the effect of his choices in the visions shown to him.
examples.translationCN: 斯克鲁奇从展现在眼前的幻象中看到了自己选择的后果。
examples.speakText: Scrooge saw the effect of his choices in the visions shown to him.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Researchers continue to measure the long-term effects of wildfire smoke on public health.
examples.translationCN: 近年研究人员持续评估野火烟雾对公共健康的长期影响。
examples.speakText: Researchers continue to measure the long-term effects of wildfire smoke on public health.

senses.meaningEN: to adjust to new conditions or change a work for a new form
senses.pattern: adapt to; adapt A for B

d06-adapt.usageNote: adapt 是“调整以适应”或“改编”，常用 adapt to；adopt 是“选择采用”或“收养”，不表示自身调整。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: New students often need time to adapt to university life.
examples.translationCN: 新生通常需要时间适应大学生活。
examples.speakText: New students often need time to adapt to university life.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Heidi adapted to mountain life and found joy in its simple routines.
examples.translationCN: 海蒂适应了山中生活，并在朴素的日常中找到快乐。
examples.speakText: Heidi adapted to mountain life and found joy in its simple routines.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Coastal cities are adapting drainage systems to heavier rainfall and rising seas.
examples.translationCN: 近年沿海城市正在调整排水系统，以应对更强降雨和海平面上升。
examples.speakText: Coastal cities are adapting drainage systems to heavier rainfall and rising seas.

senses.meaningEN: to choose to use something or legally take a child into a family

d06-adopt.usageNote: adopt 是“正式选择采用”或“收养”；adapt 是“改变以适应”或“改编”。采用方案不能写成 adapt a policy。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The department adopted a new system for booking study rooms.
examples.translationCN: 该系采用了新的自习室预约系统。
examples.speakText: The department adopted a new system for booking study rooms.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Matthew and Marilla decided to adopt Anne and make Green Gables her home.
examples.translationCN: 马修和玛丽拉决定收养安妮，让绿山墙成为她的家。
examples.speakText: Matthew and Marilla decided to adopt Anne and make Green Gables her home.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Many hospitals have adopted digital systems that help doctors review medical images.
examples.translationCN: 近年许多医院采用了帮助医生审阅医学影像的数字系统。
examples.speakText: Many hospitals have adopted digital systems that help doctors review medical images.

senses.meaningEN: related to an economy, trade, industry, or the study of these

d06-economic.usageNote: economic 表示“与经济或经济学有关”；economical 表示“节省钱或资源的”。economic car 通常不是“省油车”。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The essay compares the economic development of two coastal cities.
examples.translationCN: 这篇文章比较了两个沿海城市的经济发展。
examples.speakText: The essay compares the economic development of two coastal cities.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: The Joad family's journey was shaped by economic hardship and lost work.
examples.translationCN: 经济困境和失去工作塑造了乔德一家的旅程。
examples.speakText: The Joad family's journey was shaped by economic hardship and lost work.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Supply-chain changes have become an important part of economic planning in many countries.
examples.translationCN: 近年供应链变化已成为许多国家经济规划的重要部分。
examples.speakText: Supply-chain changes have become an important part of economic planning in many countries.

senses.meaningEN: using little money, fuel, time, or other resources

d06-economical.usageNote: economical 指“节约、不浪费、实惠的”；economic 只说明与经济或经济学有关。省油方案应说 economical。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Sharing textbooks can be an economical choice for students.
examples.translationCN: 合用教材对学生来说可以是一个省钱的选择。
examples.speakText: Sharing textbooks can be an economical choice for students.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: The March family found economical ways to celebrate despite having little money.
examples.translationCN: 尽管钱不多，马奇一家仍找到了节俭的庆祝方式。
examples.speakText: The March family found economical ways to celebrate despite having little money.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Electric buses are becoming more economical to operate as battery costs decline.
examples.translationCN: 随着电池成本下降，电动公交车的运营正变得更经济实惠。
examples.speakText: Electric buses are becoming more economical to operate as battery costs decline.

senses.meaningEN: important or influential in history

d06-historic.usageNote: historic 表示“会被历史铭记、具有重大意义的”；historical 仅表示“与历史或过去有关”，不一定重要。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The museum lecture explained why the peace agreement was historic.
examples.translationCN: 博物馆讲座解释了为何这份和平协议具有历史意义。
examples.speakText: The museum lecture explained why the peace agreement was historic.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Sydney Carton's final choice gave private courage a historic setting in revolutionary Paris.
examples.translationCN: 西德尼·卡顿最后的选择，让个人勇气置于革命时期巴黎这一历史性背景中。
examples.speakText: Sydney Carton's final choice gave private courage a historic setting in revolutionary Paris.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: The first image of a black hole was widely regarded as a historic scientific achievement.
examples.translationCN: 首张黑洞图像被广泛视为具有历史意义的科学成就。
examples.speakText: The first image of a black hole was widely regarded as a historic scientific achievement.

senses.meaningEN: related to history or events of the past

d06-historical.usageNote: historical 表示“与历史研究或过去有关”；historic 表示“具有重大历史意义”。普通旧档案是 historical records。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The article uses historical records to trace changes in the town's population.
examples.translationCN: 这篇文章利用历史记录追踪该镇人口的变化。
examples.speakText: The article uses historical records to trace changes in the town's population.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Tolstoy placed his fictional families within the historical events of the Napoleonic Wars.
examples.translationCN: 托尔斯泰把虚构家族置于拿破仑战争的历史事件之中。
examples.speakText: Tolstoy placed his fictional families within the historical events of the Napoleonic Wars.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Digitized historical newspapers are helping researchers study changes in public language.
examples.translationCN: 近年数字化历史报纸正帮助研究人员考察公共语言的变化。
examples.speakText: Digitized historical newspapers are helping researchers study changes in public language.

senses.meaningEN: belonging or relating to one particular person

d06-personal.usageNote: personal 是形容词“个人的、私人的”；personnel 是集合名词“机构人员”或“人事部门”，重音也不同。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Students should not post personal information on an open class forum.
examples.translationCN: 学生不应在公开课程论坛发布个人信息。
examples.speakText: Students should not post personal information on an open class forum.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Jane Eyre defended her personal dignity when others tried to control her future.
examples.translationCN: 当他人试图控制她的未来时，简·爱捍卫了个人尊严。
examples.speakText: Jane Eyre defended her personal dignity when others tried to control her future.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Privacy laws increasingly regulate how companies collect and use personal data.
examples.translationCN: 近年隐私法律日益规范企业收集和使用个人数据的方式。
examples.speakText: Privacy laws increasingly regulate how companies collect and use personal data.

senses.meaningEN: the people employed by an organization or its staff department

d06-personnel.usageNote: personnel 是集合名词“人员”，通常不用 personnels；personal 是形容词“个人的、私人的”。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Laboratory personnel must complete the safety course every year.
examples.translationCN: 实验室人员每年都必须完成安全课程。
examples.speakText: Laboratory personnel must complete the safety course every year.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: The naval personnel aboard the ship depended on discipline during the storm.
examples.translationCN: 暴风雨中，船上的海军人员依靠纪律维持秩序。
examples.speakText: The naval personnel aboard the ship depended on discipline during the storm.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Emergency personnel increasingly use drones to survey areas damaged by earthquakes.
examples.translationCN: 近年应急人员越来越多地使用无人机勘察地震受灾地区。
examples.speakText: Emergency personnel increasingly use drones to survey areas damaged by earthquakes.

senses.meaningEN: most important or main
the head of a school or an original sum of money

d06-principal.usageNote: principal 可表示“主要的”、校长或本金；principle 只表示“原则、原理”。可记 principal 中的 pal 是“人”。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The principal reason for changing the schedule was student safety.
examples.translationCN: 改变日程的主要原因是学生安全。
examples.speakText: The principal reason for changing the schedule was student safety.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: The school principal judged Tom Sawyer's behavior more strictly than Tom expected.
examples.translationCN: 校长对汤姆·索亚行为的评判比汤姆预想的更严格。
examples.speakText: The school principal judged Tom Sawyer's behavior more strictly than Tom expected.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: For many renewable-energy projects, the principal challenge is connecting new power to the grid.
examples.translationCN: 对许多可再生能源项目而言，主要挑战是把新增电力接入电网。
examples.speakText: For many renewable-energy projects, the principal challenge is connecting new power to the grid.

senses.meaningEN: a basic rule, belief, or scientific truth

d06-principle.usageNote: principle 是“原则、原理”；principal 指“主要的”、校长或本金。两词同音但拼写和词义不同。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Academic honesty is a basic principle of university study.
examples.translationCN: 学术诚信是大学学习的一项基本原则。
examples.speakText: Academic honesty is a basic principle of university study.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Atticus followed the principle of equal justice despite public hostility.
examples.translationCN: 尽管面对公众敌意，阿蒂克斯仍遵循平等正义的原则。
examples.speakText: Atticus followed the principle of equal justice despite public hostility.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: The principle of human oversight appears in many recent frameworks for responsible artificial intelligence.
examples.translationCN: 人工监督原则出现在近年许多负责任人工智能框架中。
examples.speakText: The principle of human oversight appears in many recent frameworks for responsible artificial intelligence.

senses.meaningEN: showing good judgment and practical understanding

d06-sensible.usageNote: sensible 指“明智、合理”；sensitive 指“敏感、易受影响或体察感受”。明智决定是 sensible decision。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: It is sensible to save a copy of your work in two places.
examples.translationCN: 把作业副本保存在两个地方是明智的。
examples.speakText: It is sensible to save a copy of your work in two places.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Elinor Dashwood offered sensible advice when emotion confused the family.
examples.translationCN: 当情绪让家人困惑时，埃莉诺·达什伍德提出了明智建议。
examples.speakText: Elinor Dashwood offered sensible advice when emotion confused the family.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Water reuse is a sensible option for many cities facing longer dry periods.
examples.translationCN: 对面临更长干旱期的许多城市而言，水资源再利用是合理选择。
examples.speakText: Water reuse is a sensible option for many cities facing longer dry periods.

senses.meaningEN: quick to notice, respond to, or be affected by small changes

d06-sensitive.usageNote: sensitive 指“敏感、易受影响或善解人意”；sensible 指“判断明智、做法合理”。光敏设备应说 sensitive。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: This measuring device is sensitive to small changes in temperature.
examples.translationCN: 这台测量设备对温度的微小变化很敏感。
examples.speakText: This measuring device is sensitive to small changes in temperature.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Marianne Dashwood was deeply sensitive to music, beauty, and disappointment.
examples.translationCN: 玛丽安·达什伍德对音乐、美和失望都感受敏锐。
examples.speakText: Marianne Dashwood was deeply sensitive to music, beauty, and disappointment.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: New ocean sensors are sensitive enough to detect subtle changes in temperature and chemistry.
examples.translationCN: 近年的新型海洋传感器足够灵敏，能探测温度和化学性质的细微变化。
examples.speakText: New ocean sensors are sensitive enough to detect subtle changes in temperature and chemistry.

senses.meaningEN: large in amount, degree, or importance

d06-considerable.usageNote: considerable 表示数量、程度“相当大”；considerate 表示人或行为“替别人着想、体贴”。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The final project requires considerable time and careful planning.
examples.translationCN: 期末项目需要大量时间和周密规划。
examples.speakText: The final project requires considerable time and careful planning.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Jean Valjean showed considerable strength while carrying another man to safety.
examples.translationCN: 冉阿让把另一个人背到安全处时展现了非凡的力量。
examples.speakText: Jean Valjean showed considerable strength while carrying another man to safety.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: The shift to electric transport requires considerable investment in charging networks.
examples.translationCN: 向电动交通转型需要对充电网络进行大量投资。
examples.speakText: The shift to electric transport requires considerable investment in charging networks.

senses.meaningEN: careful not to inconvenience or hurt other people

d06-considerate.usageNote: considerate 形容人或行为“体贴周到”，常用 considerate of；considerable 表示数量或程度“相当大”。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: It was considerate of Mei to lower her voice in the library.
examples.translationCN: 小梅在图书馆放低声音，真是替别人着想。
examples.speakText: It was considerate of Mei to lower her voice in the library.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Beth March remained considerate of her sisters even while she was ill.
examples.translationCN: 即使生病时，贝丝·马奇仍然体贴姐妹们。
examples.speakText: Beth March remained considerate of her sisters even while she was ill.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Considerate urban design includes quiet areas and step-free routes for diverse users.
examples.translationCN: 体贴周到的城市设计会为不同使用者设置安静区域和无台阶通道。
examples.speakText: Considerate urban design includes quiet areas and step-free routes for diverse users.

senses.meaningEN: to make certain that something happens or is true

d06-ensure.usageNote: ensure 表示“确保某事发生”，常接名词或 that 从句；insure 指通过保险提供经济保障。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: Check every page to ensure that your name appears on the report.
examples.translationCN: 检查每一页，确保报告上有你的名字。
examples.speakText: Check every page to ensure that your name appears on the report.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Wendy counted the children to ensure that everyone had returned safely.
examples.translationCN: 温迪数了数孩子们，以确保每个人都安全回来了。
examples.speakText: Wendy counted the children to ensure that everyone had returned safely.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Satellite operators coordinate paths to help ensure the safe use of crowded orbits.
examples.translationCN: 近年卫星运营方协调运行轨道，以帮助确保拥挤轨道的安全使用。
examples.speakText: Satellite operators coordinate paths to help ensure the safe use of crowded orbits.

senses.meaningEN: to provide or buy financial protection against loss or damage

d06-insure.usageNote: insure 通常指“投保、承保”，常用 insure something against damage；ensure 是“确保某结果”。美式英语中 insure 偶尔也可表示 ensure。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The university advised students to insure expensive equipment before travelling.
examples.translationCN: 大学建议学生在旅行前为昂贵设备投保。
examples.speakText: The university advised students to insure expensive equipment before travelling.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: A cautious merchant would insure his cargo before a dangerous sea voyage.
examples.translationCN: 谨慎的商人会在危险的海上航行前为货物投保。
examples.speakText: A cautious merchant would insure his cargo before a dangerous sea voyage.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Insurers are revising how they insure homes exposed to repeated floods and wildfires.
examples.translationCN: 近年保险公司正在调整为反复遭受洪水和野火风险的房屋承保的方式。
examples.speakText: Insurers are revising how they insure homes exposed to repeated floods and wildfires.

senses.meaningEN: a polite expression of praise, or to give such praise

d06-compliment.usageNote: compliment 含 i，表示“赞美”，常用 compliment someone on something；complement 含 e，表示“补足、相配”。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The tutor complimented Lina on the clarity of her presentation.
examples.translationCN: 导师称赞了丽娜演示的清晰度。
examples.speakText: The tutor complimented Lina on the clarity of her presentation.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Mr Collins offered Elizabeth a compliment so formal that it sounded rehearsed.
examples.translationCN: 柯林斯先生对伊丽莎白说了一句过分正式、仿佛排练过的赞美。
examples.speakText: Mr Collins offered Elizabeth a compliment so formal that it sounded rehearsed.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Athletes often compliment volunteers whose work keeps major competitions running smoothly.
examples.translationCN: 运动员常常称赞志愿者，因为他们的工作让大型赛事顺利运行。
examples.speakText: Athletes often compliment volunteers whose work keeps major competitions running smoothly.

senses.meaningEN: something that completes or matches another thing, or to do so

d06-complement.usageNote: complement 含 e，表示“补足、相配”；compliment 含 i，表示“赞美”。complement 直接接宾语，不用 complement to 作动词结构。

examples.label: CET-4 Context
examples.text: The practical workshop complements the theory taught in lectures.
examples.translationCN: 实践工作坊补充了讲座中教授的理论。
examples.speakText: The practical workshop complements the theory taught in lectures.

examples.label: Literature
examples.text: Holmes's sharp reasoning and Watson's humane attention complement each other.
examples.translationCN: 福尔摩斯敏锐的推理与华生充满人情味的关注相辅相成。
examples.speakText: Holmes's sharp reasoning and Watson's humane attention complement each other.

examples.label: World Now
examples.text: Battery storage can complement solar and wind power when weather conditions change.
examples.translationCN: 天气条件变化时，电池储能可以补充太阳能和风能。
examples.speakText: Battery storage can complement solar and wind power when weather conditions change.
